title: Rivian's Q4 Achievement and 2025 Outlook
text: Rivian Automotive achieved its first-ever gross profit in Q4 2024, reaching $170 million compared to a $660 million gross loss in the same quarter of 2023. Record revenues were driven by regulatory credits, software services, and higher vehicle prices. Cost reductions of $31,000 per vehicle were achieved year-over-year. Loss-per-share was narrower compared to year-ago results. For 2025, the company expects to maintain modest gross profit but faces headwinds. Management projects deliveries of 46,000-51,000 vehicles, down from 51,579 units delivered in 2024. This guidance reflects concerns about potential changes in government policies, including shifts in EV incentives and tariffs. The company finalized a partnership with Volkswagen worth up to $5.8 billion, providing $3.5 billion in funding. Upcoming R2, R3, and R3X models are targeting budget-conscious buyers. An approved $6.6 billion federal loan for Georgia manufacturing plant's future is uncertain under Trump administration. Cash reserves declined from $7.9 billion to $5.3 billion. The stock carries a Hold recommendation from most analysts, with concerns about cash burn and political uncertainty surrounding EV policy.
